channel.title: Unidentified Flying Object on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Unidentified Flying Object on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Hoaxes Related to Unidentified Flying Objects
item.description:
Numerous hoaxes related to the study of unidentified flying objects have been perpetrated throughout history. These include practical jokes like a balloon with a burning basket in 1897 and a turkey vulture with a burning cotton ball, which led to a school fire. Other hoaxes involved fabricated accounts of airships, such as one where a cow was reportedly lifted by a ship, and the controversial “alien autopsy” film footage that was later revealed to be a hoax. Photographic hoaxes, like those of George Adamski and a purported alien at Ilkley Moor, have also been exposed.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_UFO-related_hoaxes

item.title: Center for UFO Studies (CUFOS)
item.description:
The Center for UFO Studies (CUFOS) is a privately funded UFO research group founded in 1973 by J. Allen Hynek. Hynek, who had previously consulted for the U.S. Air Force’s Project Blue Book, established CUFOS to continue the scientific study of UFO reports after the project’s closure. The organization maintains a library and archives of UFO-related materials and compiles and analyzes UFO incident reports from around the world. CUFOS is now based in Chicago, Illinois, and its case files were moved to Albuquerque, New Mexico, in 2020.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Center_for_UFO_Studies

item.title: Civilian Saucer Intelligence
item.description:
Civilian Saucer Intelligence (CSI) was an independent UFO research group founded in New York City in 1954. Unlike many amateur groups, CSI conducted rigorous investigations and published a well-regarded quarterly newsletter. The organization was critical of contactees but distinct in its investigation of close encounters involving alleged animate beings. CSI’s core personnel included Ted Bloecher, Isabel Davis, and Alexander Mebane, and the group was instrumental in translating French ufologist Aimé Michel’s books into English. CSI was defunct by 1959, though Davis and Bloecher remained active in UFO research.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Civilian_Saucer_Intelligence

item.title: Gilliland's Ranch
item.description:
Gilliland’s Ranch, also known as ECETI Ranch and Sattva Sanctuary, is located in Trout Lake, Washington, at the base of Mount Adams. The property owner, James Gilliland, claims to have established contact with extraterrestrial intelligence and has hosted UFO sighting events since 2003. Gilliland reports frequent UFO sightings and “unexplained light shows” at the location.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilliland%27s_Ranch

item.title: Green fireballs
item.description:
Green fireballs are a type of unidentified flying object reported since the early 1950s, primarily in the southwestern United States. Early reports from late 1948 described them as bright green balls of fire or like huge green meteors. While some ufologists suggest an extraterrestrial origin, mainstream explanations include natural bolides. Investigations in the late 1940s and early 1950s, including Project Twinkle, were unable to definitively identify the origin, with official conclusions leaning towards natural phenomena.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Green_fireballs
